primary_sources.notes: Consolidated revenue ~₹145,000 Cr. Network costs ~₹55,000 Cr (38%), Employee ~₹10,000 Cr (6.9%), License/spectrum fees ~₹20,000 Cr (13.8%), D&A ~₹35,000 Cr (24%).

primary_sources.source_name: Reliance Jio FY25 Annual Report (Reliance Industries subsidiary — segment data BSE: 500325)
primary_sources.notes: Jio segment revenue ~₹100,000 Cr. Network operating costs ~35%, Staff ~5%, License fees ~14%.

primary_sources.source_name: TRAI Annual Report 2023-24 (Telecom Regulatory Authority of India)
primary_sources.notes: Published telecom industry cost benchmarks: network O&M, AGR (Adjusted Gross Revenue) based license fees, spectrum usage charges as % of AGR.
Tower infrastructure company. Revenue ~₹28,000 Cr. Energy cost ~₹10,000 Cr (35.7%), Employee ~₹1,000 Cr (3.6%), Maintenance ~₹3,500 Cr (12.5%).
India telecom industry cost structure analysis: network operating cost, spectrum, and ARPU data.

source_metadata.methodology: P25/P50/P75 derived from FY24-25 annual reports of 3 India telecom entities (Airtel, Jio, Indus Towers). TRAI published data used for licence fee and regulatory cost cross-validation. Categories as % of total revenue. Jio represents world-class low-cost structure (P25); Vodafone Idea represents stressed P90.

OUTSOURCED.source_note: Network operating costs: tower rental, inter-connect charges, roaming, network O&M. Airtel FY25: ~38%, Jio FY25: ~35%. Indus Towers: energy + maintenance ~48% of tower revenue.

HR.source_note: Employee costs % of revenue. Jio FY25: ~5%, Airtel FY25: 6.9%, Indus Towers: 3.6%. TRAI: industry average 5-8% of revenues for large operators.

POWER_ENERGY.source_note: Energy for network operations (towers, data centres). Indus Towers FY25: energy 35.7% of tower revenue. Telecom operators: energy 10-15% of total revenue. TRAI: energy cost management as key profitability driver.

FIN_SVCS.source_note: Licence fees + spectrum usage charges (SUC). TRAI: AGR-linked licence fee 8% + SUC 3-5% = 11-13% of AGR. DoT regulatory levies. Airtel FY25: licence fees ~13.8% of revenue.

IT.source_note: IT/OSS/BSS systems, cloud, cybersecurity. GSMA India 2024: telecom IT 3-5% of revenue. Network virtualisation (NFV/SDN) investment increasing IT spend. Jio cloud-native stack at lower end.

PLANT_MAINTENANCE.source_note: Network R&M, tower maintenance. Indus Towers FY25: maintenance 12.5% of tower revenue. Telecom operators: 3-5% of revenue on network maintenance.

MARKETING.source_note: Sales, customer acquisition, brand. Airtel FY25: sales & marketing ~3.5%, Jio: ~2.5% (digital-first). TRAI: industry average 3-5% of revenue on S&M.

FACILITIES.source_note: Corporate and customer service offices. INTERNAL_CALIBRATION.
